author: y4rr
content:
Phase 2A complete. Added ferrotorch-core::masked module (~470 LOC, 16 tests) wrapping ferray-ma 0.3.1.

Added type:
- MaskedTensor<T: Float> = (data: Tensor<T>, mask: Vec<bool>, fill_value: T)
- Mask convention: TORCH semantics (mask[i]=true means VALID). Documented; opposite of numpy.ma which uses true=invalid. Internal bridge to ferray-ma negates at the boundary so the public API is torch-shaped while the implementation borrows ferray-ma's well-tested ops.

Public API:
- MaskedTensor::new(data, mask) — torch-style constructor
- MaskedTensor::from_data(data) — all-valid
- MaskedTensor::with_fill_value(v) — builder for fill value
- accessors: data(), mask(), fill_value(), shape(), numel(), count_valid(), count_masked()
- filled() / to_tensor() — substitute fill_value at masked positions
- to_ferray<U>() — escape hatch returning ferray_ma::MaskedArray for the wider ferray-ma op surface (var/std, masked sort, ufunc support)

Free functions:
- masked_where(data, condition) — numpy-style: condition=true masks OUT (i.e. internal mask = !condition)
- masked_invalid(data) — masks NaN/Inf entries
- masked_equal(data, value) — masks entries equal to value
- masked_sum / masked_mean / masked_min / masked_max / masked_count — reductions over valid entries; reductions return scalar (0-d) tensors. masked_mean returns NaN if every entry is masked (matches torch.masked).

Bridge correctness:
- to_ferray_round_trip_mean_matches_inhouse test confirms our in-house masked_mean and ferray-ma's MaskedArray::mean() agree to 1e-12 on the same input — verifies the mask-inversion bridge is semantically correct.

GPU discipline (apply /rust-gpu-discipline):
- Every constructor and reduction calls is_cuda() at entry; GPU input returns NotImplementedOnCuda with the op name.
- ferray-ma is CPU-only; we never silently bounce GPU data through host. Reductions use a single-pass loop directly over data_vec rather than routing through ferray-ma for the common cases — avoids the f64 round-trip when T is f32/f64 and stays simpler.
- Forbidden patterns grep clean: no .to(Device::Cpu) inside compute, no tensor_to_cpu/cpu_to_gpu sandwich.
- Filed #597 to track GPU MaskedTensor (kernel-based reductions via where_cond + sum/mean — primitives already exist in ferrotorch-gpu).

Bool tensor question (audit issue brief flagged this): ferrotorch's Float trait doesn't include bool; we use Vec<bool> for the mask backing store rather than Tensor<bool>. This is a pragmatic choice for Phase 2A — when integer/bool element types land via ferray-core (#596), the mask can move to a Tensor<bool> and reductions can dispatch through GPU kernels.

Tests: 16 new in masked::tests covering construction (length-mismatch rejection), constructor variants (masked_where/invalid/equal), filled / to_tensor with default and explicit fill values, all 5 reductions (sum/mean/min/max/count), all-masked NaN behaviour for mean, ferray-ma bridge round-trip cross-check, and CPU-tensor smoke for every constructor.

Workspace tests: 4566 passed, 0 failed, 100 ignored (was 4550; +16 new masked tests). Closes #569 (Phase 2A). Follow-up: #597 for GPU paths.
